title: absolute_paths

title: allow_attributes_without_reason

title: almost_complete_range

title: almost_swapped

title: arbitrary_source_item_ordering

title: arc_with_non_send_sync

title: arithmetic_side_effects

title: as_pointer_underscore

title: as_ptr_cast_mut

title: as_underscore

title: await_holding_refcell_ref

title: bad_bit_mask

title: big_endian_bytes

title: bind_instead_of_map
description: Checks for usage of _.and_then(|x| Some(y)), _.and_then(|x| Ok(y)) or _.or_else(|x| Err(y)).

structuralHints.treeSitterQuery:
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    value: (_)
    field: (field_identifier) @method
    (#any-of? @method "and_then" "or_else"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (closure_expression
      body: [
        (call_expression
          function: (identifier) @func
          (#any-of? @func "Some" "Ok" "Err"))
        (block
          (call_expression
            function: (identifier) @func
            (#any-of? @func "Some" "Ok" "Err")))
      ])) @offender)

title: bool_to_int_with_if

title: borrow_as_ptr

title: borrow_deref_ref

title: borrow_interior_mutable_const

title: box_collection

title: branches_sharing_code

title: builtin_type_shadow

title: cast_possible_truncation
description: Checks for casts between numeric types that may truncate large values. This is expected behavior, so the cast is Allow by default. It suggests user either explicitly ignore the lint, or use try_from() and handle the truncation, default, or panic explicitly.

title: cast_possible_wrap
description: Checks for casts from an unsigned type to a signed type of the same size, or possibly smaller due to target-dependent integers. Performing such a cast is a no-op for the compiler (that is, nothing is changed at the bit level), and the binary representation of the value is reinterpreted. This can cause wrapping if the value is too big for the target signed type. However, the cast works as defined, so this lint is Allow by default.

title: cast_precision_loss
description: Checks for casts from any numeric type to a float type where the receiving type cannot store all values from the original type without rounding errors. This possible rounding is to be expected, so this lint is Allow by default.

title: cast_ptr_alignment
description: Checks for casts, using as or pointer::cast, from a less strictly aligned pointer to a more strictly aligned pointer.

title: cast_slice_from_raw_parts

Anything is fair game, create new classes, new methods, and rename tests.

Many tools and frameworks are capable of generating SQL. However, given the rules of the relational model, or the heterogeneous attributes of the tool/framework to work with as many databases as possible, these tools or frameworks may not respect case sensitivity on table or column names. Hence there is a risk that one tool uses, for example, all upper case characters for the table name, while another uses all lower case characters. If you use case sensitive names one or the other tool will generate SQL that will fail.

### Added complexity for one-off hand-written SQL
Most users who connect to a database and write a quick SQL query to answer a question for the manager or similar don't write well-formatted SQL. Most likely, they will write all lower case SQL and don't bother much about how it looks. By using case sensitive names you add unnecessary complexity to their SQL statement attempts.

examples.bad:
- Todos los artículos (`item`) tienen una propiedad `SellIn` que denota el número de días que tenemos para venderlo
- Todos los artículos (`item`) tienen una propiedad `Quality` que denota cúan valioso es el artículo
- Al final de cada día, nuestro sistema decrementa ambos valores para cada artículo mediante el método `updateQuality`

Bastante simple, ¿no? Bueno, ahora es donde se pone interesante:

* Artikulu guztiek (`Item`) `sellIn` izeneko propietate bat dute berau saltzeko ditugun egunen kopurua adierazten duena.
* Artikulu guztiek `quality` izeneko propietate bat dute artikulu hori zein baliotsua den adierazten duena.
* Egunaren amaieran, gure sistemak artikulu bakoitzerako bi balio horiek gutxitzen ditu `updateQuality` metodoaren bitartez.

Nahiko sinplea, ezta? Beno, orain da interesgarri jartzen denean:

- Todos os artigos (`item`) teñen unha propiedade `SellIn` que indica o número de días que temos para vendelo
- Todos os artigos (`item`) teñen unha propiedade `Quality` que indica o valioso que é o artigo
- Ao final de cada día, o noso sistema actualiza ambos valores para cada artigo mediante o método `updateQuality`

Bastante sinxelo, non? Pois agora é cando se pon interesante:

- Tutti i prodotti (`Item`) hanno una proprietà `sellIn` che indica quanti giorni mancano alla data di scadenza.
- Tutti i prodotti (`Item`) hanno una proprietà `quality` che denota il valore dell'articolo.
- Alla fine di ogni giornata il nostro sistema decrementa entrambe le proprietà per ogni prodotto tramite il metodo `updateQuality`

Abbastanza semplice, vero? Bene, è da qui che la cosa si fa interessante:

* Todos os itens (classe `Item`) possuem uma propriedade chamada `SellIn` que informa o número de dias que temos para vende-lo
* Todos os itens possuem uma propriedade chamada `quality` que informa o quão valioso é o item.
* No final do dia, nosso sistema decrementa os valores das propriedades `SellIn` e `quality` de cada um dos itens do estoque através do método `updateQuality`.

Bastante simples, não é? Bem, agora que as coisas ficam interessantes:

title: useless_asref
description: Checks for usage of .as_ref() or .as_mut() where the types before and after the call are the same.

title: useless_concat
description: Checks that the concat! macro has at least two arguments.

title: useless_conversion
description: Checks for Into, TryInto, From, TryFrom, or IntoIter calls which uselessly convert to the same type.

structuralHints.treeSitterQuery:
(call_expression
    function: (generic_function
      function: (scoped_identifier
        path: (identifier)
        name: (identifier) @method_name
        (#any-of? @method_name "into" "try_into" "from" "try_from" "into_iter"))
      type_arguments: (type_arguments
        (type_identifier) @target_type))
    arguments: (arguments
      (identifier) @arg))

title: useless_format
description: Checks for the use of format!("string literal with no argument") and format!("{}", foo) where foo is a string.

title: useless_let_if_seq
description: Checks for variable declarations immediately followed by a conditional affectation.

title: useless_nonzero_new_unchecked
description: Checks for NonZero*::new_unchecked() being used in a const context.

title: useless_transmute
description: Checks for transmutes to the original type of the object and transmutes that could be a cast.

structuralHints.treeSitterQuery:
(call_expression
  function: (generic_function
    function: (identifier) @func_name
    type_arguments: (type_arguments
      [
        (generic_type
          type: (type_identifier) @from_type
          type_arguments: (type_arguments
            (type_identifier) @inner_type))
        (type_identifier) @from_type
      ]
      [
        (generic_type
          type: (type_identifier) @to_type
          type_arguments: (type_arguments
            (type_identifier) @inner_type))
        (type_identifier) @to_type
      ]))
  arguments: (arguments
    [
      (identifier)
      (field_expression)
      (call_expression)
    ]))

title: useless_vec
description: Checks for usage of vec![..] when using [..] would be possible.

title: vec_init_then_push
description: Checks for calls to push immediately after creating a new Vec.

title: vec_resize_to_zero

title: verbose_file_reads
description: Checks for usage of File::read_to_end and File::read_to_string.

title: volatile_composites
description: This lint warns when volatile load/store operations (write_volatile/read_volatile) are applied to composite types.

title: waker_clone_wake

title: while_float
description: Checks for while loops comparing floating point values.

title: while_immutable_condition
description: Checks whether variables used within while loop condition can be (and are) mutated in the body.

title: while_let_loop

title: while_let_on_iterator
description: Checks for while let expressions on iterators.
